premises.content: In twisted bilayer graphene, the Bistritzer-MacDonald continuum moire-band model supplies the noninteracting low-energy band structure, and the Dirac-point band Fermi velocity v_F*(theta) from that model is treated as the bare input velocity for subsequent low-energy many-body and phonon-transport calculations [@DasSarma2020].
premises.label: gcn_671ceef053a64aa7

premises.content: For twisted bilayer graphene in the noninteracting Bistritzer-MacDonald continuum moire-band calculation, with twist angle theta and largest magic angle theta_M measured in degrees and monolayer graphene velocity v_F about 1e8 cm/s, the computed low-energy Dirac-point Fermi velocity v_F*(theta) for 0 < theta < 3 degrees is usefully approximated by v_F*(theta) ~= 0.5 * |theta - theta_M| * v_F [@DasSarma2020].
premises.label: gcn_d3808439ccf6496b
